category_description: Overwritten amount — OCR misses, Vision LLM catches

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: Amount field alteration detected. Ink density analysis reveals two distinct writing layers in the amount figures field. NOTE: OCR reads the overwritten value as valid — only Vision LLM ink-layer analysis reveals the fraud. This is a classic cheque washing/overwrite fraud.

annotations.detail: Ink pressure variance: original strokes at 0.8N, overwrite at 1.4N. Correction fluid UV signature positive.
Handwriting baseline inconsistency. Words field shows erasure marks beneath current text.

ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: Original ₹9,000 — Nine Thousand Only ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: Date field tampering detected. Year portion of date field shows ink inconsistency with the rest of the document. Digit '2026' has a distinctly different ink absorption profile compared to the day and month portions, which appear original. Correction fluid (Whiteout/Tipp-Ex) residue detected beneath year digits — UV channel shows characteristic bright bloom. Original year reconstructed as '2024', making this cheque stale (issued 15-01-2024, now >90 days old). The year was altered from '2024' to '2026' to circumvent the RBI stale cheque rule. NOTE: OCR reads date as 15-01-2026 and PASSES the stale check. Only Vision LLM ink analysis reveals the year was tampered.

annotations.detail: Year digits '2026' show: (1) higher ink density than remainder of date, (2) correction fluid UV bloom, (3) slight misalignment vs date baseline. Original year '2024' reconstructed from residual ink traces.

ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: Original date: 15-01-2024 — STALE (>90 days old). Year fraudulently altered 2024→2026. ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES
ocr_vs_vision.note: OCR is a character reader — it cannot detect that '2026' was written over '2024'. Vision LLM's spatial ink analysis catches the physical tampering that OCR is blind to.

category_description: CANCELLED stamp — OCR misses, Vision LLM catches

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: CANCELLED instrument detected. A diagonal 'CANCELLED' bank stamp in red ink is overlaid across the face of this cheque. The stamp pattern is consistent with an official bank rubber stamp used to void instruments. Stamp dimensions: ~180mm diagonal, text height ~12mm, serif font consistent with standard bank voids. Red ink hue: 0°–8° (pure red channel dominant, green/blue <0.15). The stamp covers approximately 65% of the cheque face. CTS-2010 Rule 8.3 and RBI Circular DPSS.CO.CHD.No.1404/04.07.05/2012-13: Cancelled instruments must not be presented for clearing. NOTE: OCR successfully extracted all text fields from beneath the stamp overlay — OCR character recognition is not affected by the stamp but has no stamp-detection capability. Only Vision LLM spatial analysis identifies the void stamp.

annotations.detail: Diagonal CANCELLED stamp detected. Red ink (R:220, G:32, B:28). Stamp text: 'CANCELLED'. Orientation: 30° diagonal. Coverage: 65% of instrument face. Official bank stamp pattern confirmed.

ocr_vs_vision.ocr_reads: All text fields extracted successfully. Amount, payee, date — all valid. ← OCR PASSES
ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: CANCELLED stamp across instrument face. Void cheque. Must not be cleared. ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES
ocr_vs_vision.note: OCR reads text. It does not understand that a red diagonal stamp means VOID. Vision LLM's document understanding recognises the stamp as a cancellation mark — a capability impossible with OCR alone.

category_description: Overwritten amount — OCR misses, Vision LLM catches

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: Amount field alteration detected. Ink density analysis reveals two distinct writing layers in the amount figures field. Primary (original) layer shows '₹3,500' with ink absorption profile consistent with original cheque fill. Secondary overwrite layer detected with 93% confidence — correction fluid residue (UV signature detected) beneath overwrite. Amount words field was also re-written over erasure. Original instrument value: ₹3,500 (Three Thousand Five Hundred Only). Fraudulently altered to: ₹35,000 (Thirty Five Thousand Only). NOTE: OCR reads the overwritten value as valid — only Vision LLM ink-layer analysis reveals the fraud. This is a classic cheque washing/overwrite fraud.

annotations.detail: Ink pressure variance: original strokes at 0.8N, overwrite at 1.4N. Correction fluid UV signature positive.
Handwriting baseline inconsistency. Words field shows erasure marks beneath current text.

ocr_vs_vision.ocr_reads: ₹35,000 — Thirty Five Thousand Only ← OCR PASSES
ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: Original ₹3,500 — Three Thousand Five Hundred Only ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES
ocr_vs_vision.note: THIS IS THE KEY VALUE DIFFERENTIATOR: OCR cannot detect ink-layer anomalies. Vision LLM spatial reasoning identifies the original ink layer beneath the overwrite, catching a fraud that would pass conventional OCR processing.

category_description: Tampered date (year changed) — OCR misses, Vision LLM catches

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: Date field tampering detected. Year portion of date field shows ink inconsistency with the rest of the document. Digit '2026' has a distinctly different ink absorption profile compared to the day and month portions, which appear original. Correction fluid (Whiteout/Tipp-Ex) residue detected beneath year digits — UV channel shows characteristic bright bloom. Original year reconstructed as '2024', making this cheque stale (issued 14-04-2024, now >90 days old). The year was altered from '2024' to '2026' to circumvent the RBI stale cheque rule. NOTE: OCR reads date as 14-04-2026 and PASSES the stale check. Only Vision LLM ink analysis reveals the year was tampered.

annotations.detail: Year digits '2026' show: (1) higher ink density than remainder of date, (2) correction fluid UV bloom, (3) slight misalignment vs date baseline. Original year '2024' reconstructed from residual ink traces.

ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: Original date: 14-04-2024 — STALE (>90 days old). Year fraudulently altered 2024→2026. ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES
ocr_vs_vision.note: OCR is a character reader — it cannot detect that '2026' was written over '2024'. Vision LLM's spatial ink analysis catches the physical tampering that OCR is blind to.

category_description: CANCELLED stamp — OCR misses, Vision LLM catches

vision_llm_output.analysis: CRITICAL ALERT: CANCELLED instrument detected. A diagonal 'CANCELLED' bank stamp in red ink is overlaid across the face of this cheque. The stamp pattern is consistent with an official bank rubber stamp used to void instruments. Stamp dimensions: ~180mm diagonal, text height ~12mm, serif font consistent with standard bank voids. Red ink hue: 0°–8° (pure red channel dominant, green/blue <0.15). The stamp covers approximately 65% of the cheque face. CTS-2010 Rule 8.3 and RBI Circular DPSS.CO.CHD.No.1404/04.07.05/2012-13: Cancelled instruments must not be presented for clearing. NOTE: OCR successfully extracted all text fields from beneath the stamp overlay — OCR character recognition is not affected by the stamp but has no stamp-detection capability. Only Vision LLM spatial analysis identifies the void stamp.

annotations.detail: Diagonal CANCELLED stamp detected. Red ink (R:220, G:32, B:28). Stamp text: 'CANCELLED'. Orientation: 30° diagonal. Coverage: 65% of instrument face. Official bank stamp pattern confirmed.

ocr_vs_vision.ocr_reads: All text fields extracted successfully. Amount, payee, date — all valid. ← OCR PASSES
ocr_vs_vision.vision_llm_detects: CANCELLED stamp across instrument face. Void cheque. Must not be cleared. ← VISION LLM OVERRIDES
ocr_vs_vision.note: OCR reads text. It does not understand that a red diagonal stamp means VOID. Vision LLM's document understanding recognises the stamp as a cancellation mark — a capability impossible with OCR alone.
